NIGHT'S WATCH
The shield that guards the realms of men.

One citizen's scam report is a network sensor.

A consent-first fraud-network intelligence platform. Detect a scam from text, link its extracted identifiers into a fraud-network graph, and produce a court-admissible evidence pack that names the ring and ranks its kingpin.

Submission document · full system Detect → Link → Prove 246 tests green code_version 9f7af0f Vybhav Chaturvedi · IIM Calcutta · IIT Kharagpur
SummaryScoringProblem ArchitectureSchema DetectLinkProve GuardrailsValidationLead time CounterfeitScaleFoundations EngineeringThe appDemo Q&A
Executive summary

What it is, in one screen

One product, three components, one integration seam. A citizen forwards a suspicious message; the tool returns a verdict and guidance — and quietly emits a structured report. Those reports feed a fraud-network graph that clusters reused identifiers into rings and ranks the controller across them. Every flagged ring exports a hash-stamped evidence pack.

1.0 / 1.0
Ring recovery P/R across 20 seeds (± 0.0)
20 / 20
Kingpin ranked #1 on independently seeded networks
0 / 4,825
Real-SMS false positives (UCI ham)
4 / 55,835
Held-out FP (NUS corpus, zero tuning)
28
Victims preventable after the ring flags at report 2
The load-bearing insight

The detector's job for the graph is structured entity extraction, not classification. A "scam: yes, 0.9" verdict is useless to a network graph — the graph runs on the extracted identifiers (payee_upi, phone, account, device). Get the shared report schema identifier-first and Detect→Link is one real product; get it verdict-first and it is two demos stapled together.

How this is scored

Answered against the evaluation focus, criterion by criterion

The final round scores five things, in its own stated order. Each row below is answered with a number produced by code in this repo, and each carries its honest posture — a measured figure with its caveat beats an asserted one.

#Judged criterion (rubric wording)How Night's Watch answers it
1 Counterfeit detection accuracy across denominations and print quality Note-image CV is scoped out and stated, not faked (no labelled genuine-vs-fake corpus to measure against honestly). The circulation-intelligence half is built and measured: src/counterfeit/ re-points the spine to seizure records — ring recovery 1.0 / 1.0 P/R over 20 seeds, courier kingpin #1 in 20/20. See § Counterfeit.
2 Digital-arrest detection precision and recall Two numbers, both reported: rules floor P/R/F1 = 1.0 (circular by construction), LLM path 0.82 / 0.90 / 0.86 on a balanced held-out sample (never tuned on the templates). See § Detect.
3 Fraud-network detection lead time before mass victimisation Replayed from real arrival order: ring flags at report 2, 28 subsequent victims preventable, ₹32.70 L (89.7%) of the loss arrives after the flag. See § Lead time.
4 False-positive rate for citizen-facing tools (must be very low) Two independent real-SMS corpora: UCI 0 / 4,825 ham (tuning caveat stated), NUS 4 / 55,835 = 0.01% (truly held out, zero tuning). Plus the legit-hub guardrail, so "low FP" holds at the ring level too. See § Detect / § Guardrails.
5 Auditability of intelligence packages for legal admissibility The Layer 1 / Layer 2 split: deterministic hard-connection clusters are evidence; the kingpin score is labelled a lead, not proof. Per-incident + pack SHA-256, methodology stamped with the ring's actual parameters, BSA 2023 §63 certificate framing, real code_version. See § Prove.

The four mandated deliverables

  • ✓Working prototype. The Streamlit app — Live, Command centre, Counterfeit, Lead time — runs the full pipeline offline on the rules floor; guarded by 26 app-layer tests on both classifier paths.
  • ✓Presentation deck. A 12-slide dark deck built on the mandated headings; every quantitative claim traces to a data/processed/*.json artifact.
  • ✓Architecture diagram. The horizontal Detect→Link→Prove flow with the consent and answer-key boundaries marked (reproduced in § Architecture below).
  • ✓Demo video. The rehearsed five-beat flow (§ Demo), recorded as the live-failure backup.

This document is the written companion to those four — the full system in one place, with the running interface shown as it renders and every figure traceable to the code that produced it.

The problem

Scams are reported one victim at a time. They are run as rings.

The number in the brief

₹1,776 crore was lost to digital-arrest scams alone, in nine months — the exact pretext the hero message uses. The loss is not one confused victim at a time; it is the throughput of an organised operation running the same script against thousands of people in parallel.

The gap today

Digital-arrest, parcel/customs, KYC, lottery and relative-in-distress scams are reported individually to 1930 / NCRP. Each report is triaged as an isolated incident. The coordination between them — the same payee UPI, the same mule account, the same handset behind fifty "different" scams — is invisible until long after mass victimisation.

The reframe

Treat each citizen report as a sensor reading. Extract its identifiers, drop them into a shared graph, and the ring assembles itself from hard shared connections. The first two reports naming a mule are enough to flag the ring — before the other twenty-eight victims ever transfer a rupee.

Covers every text-based pretext uniformly, because the detector reads text — SMS, WhatsApp forwards, call transcripts.

Architecture

Detect → Link → Prove

⟢ Consent boundary. The citizen submits text. Nothing is tapped, tracked, or pulled from the device — no call interception, no location.
Sensor

Detect

Classify scam-vs-legit with a red-flag breakdown, and extract typed identifiers → one identifier-first Report.

Intelligence

Link

Reports become nodes; reused identifiers become edges. Connected components on hard connections are candidate rings; centrality across rings ranks the kingpin.

Evidence

Prove

Layer 1 is the deterministic hard-connection subgraph — the proof. Layer 2 is the kingpin score — a lead, explicitly not proof. Both ship in a hashed pack.

⌀ Answer-key boundary. Synthetic reports carry a ground-truth (_gt) block — planted ring id and kingpin flag. It is stripped before the model ever sees a report and is read only by the validator, never by the detector, graph, or app.

The schema is the single blocking dependency, and it is frozen. Every entities value is a potential graph node; shared values become edges. Normalisation is fixed (UPI lowercased, phone → +91 E.164, account/IFSC uppercased), so two reports that name the same payee always collide on the same node.

The contract · frozen first

One identifier-first report, and the whole product holds together

The shared Report schema is the single blocking dependency, so it was frozen before any component was built. It is identifier-first on purpose: the verdict is for the citizen, but the entities block is what the graph runs on. Every value in it is a potential graph node; two reports that name the same value collide on the same node and become an edge.

{
  "report_id": "uuid",
  "timestamp": "ISO-8601",
  "channel": "sms | whatsapp | call_transcript | email",
  "raw_text": "…",
  "verdict":  { "is_scam": true, "confidence": 0.0, "red_flags": ["urgency", "…"] },
  "entities": {                    // ← the graph runs on THIS, not on verdict
    "payee_upi": ["fraud@okhdfc"],
    "phone":     ["+9198…"],
    "account":   ["…"], "ifsc": ["…"],
    "amount":    12000, "urls": ["…"], "device_hint": "…",
    "scam_type": "digital_arrest | parcel | kyc | lottery | relative_distress"
  },
  "extraction_confidence": 0.0,
  // answer-key fields — present ONLY in synthetic data, NEVER shown to the model:
  "_gt": { "ring_id": "R07", "is_kingpin_incident": false }
}
Why identifier-first is load-bearing

A network graph cannot use "scam: yes, 0.9". It links on the extracted payee_upi, phone, account, device. Get the schema identifier-first and Detect→Link is one product; get it verdict-first and it is two demos stapled together.

Normalisation is fixed

UPI lowercased; phone → +91 E.164; account/IFSC uppercased and trimmed. So the same payee written two ways still lands on one node — the collision the whole graph depends on is deterministic, not best-effort.

The _gt block is stripped before the detector, graph, or app ever sees a report; only the validator reads it. That boundary is what lets us prove the ring recovery is correct without the model ever peeking at the answer.

Component 01 · the sensor

Detect

Raw text in; a verdict, red flags, guidance, and a set of typed identifiers out. An LLM (Groq, llama-3.3-70b-versatile) does the fuzzy reading; a deterministic rules-and-regex floor runs underneath it, so the tool is fully functional with no API key and no internet — which is also the configuration it must survive on stage.

Classify

Scam-vs-legit with a six-signal red-flag breakdown: urgency, authority impersonation, payment demand, threat, link, secrecy.

Extract

Regex for structured identifiers (UPI, Indian phone, account, IFSC, URL, amount) plus the LLM for the fuzzy ones. Positioned spans drive the in-bubble highlight, so the UI can never claim a link the graph does not run on.

Emit

One Report conforming to the frozen schema. Verdict + guidance go to the citizen; identifiers go to the graph. Hinglish (romanised Hindi) templates covered.

Digital-arrest detection — precision & recall

The rubric names this as a scored criterion, so it is measured two ways, and both are reported with their honest posture.

Rules floor · deterministic
1.00
Precision
1.00
Recall
1.00
F1

56 planted digital-arrest messages pooled across 20 seeds — 0 FP / 0 FN. Circular by construction: the keyword lists were designed to separate these very templates, so this means "the method recovers the planted answer key", not a real-world claim.

LLM path · non-circular
0.82
Precision
0.90
Recall
0.86
F1

Balanced 80-message sample (40 DA + 40 non-DA), never used to tune a keyword. Rules-vs-LLM agreement 0.89. The 4 false negatives are the one DA template that literally says "a parcel booked under your Aadhaar", which the LLM reasonably reads as parcel/customs.

False-positive rate on real human SMS

The citizen-facing number that actually matters is how often the tool cries wolf on genuine messages. Measured on two independent real corpora — never on our own synthetic data.

UCI SMS Spam Collection

0 / 4,825 ham

Tuning caveat stated plainly: the rate was 6/4,825 at first run; the eval then drove two principled keyword fixes, so UCI is now the regression harness for those fixes, not an untouched set.

NUS SMS Corpus · truly held out

4 / 55,835 = 0.01%

55,835 real Singlish/English SMS. Zero tuning done in response — the number is frozen as measured. Two independent real corpora, one honestly circular, one not, both near zero.

Component 02 · the intelligence

Link

Each report contributes nodes for its entities; two reports that reuse a UPI / phone / account / device are joined by a shared-entity edge. Connected components on those hard connections are candidate rings (Layer 1, deterministic). Centrality across the ring-union graph ranks the node most central to multiple rings — the kingpin (Layer 2, a lead).

Seeded ring portfolio · seed 0, hub-degree cap 40
R0000digital arrest
30 · ₹36.45 L
R0001KYC update
14 · ₹22.52 L
R0002lottery prize
9 · ₹14.94 L
R0003parcel / customs
6 · ₹6.72 L
R0004loan app
5 · ₹9.11 L
R0005investment
4 · ₹7.63 L

Bar length = reported incidents; label = reported loss. Note R0004 (5 incidents, ₹9.11 L) carries more loss than R0003 (6 incidents, ₹6.72 L) — incident count and harm are not the same axis, which is why a public-safety view thinks in rupees, not nodes.

LAYER 1 · STRUCTURAL PROOF · DETERMINISTIC

Rings from hard connections

Connected components on reused identifiers. No learning, no threshold on the evidence itself — this is the layer a court can audit. The hub-degree cap is the only knob, and it is a stated guardrail, not a tuned model.

LAYER 2 · KINGPIN LEAD · AI, NOT PROOF

Centrality across rings

Degree / betweenness / eigenvector over the ring-union graph. The launderer bridging several rings surfaces here by centrality — a prioritisation lead for investigators, with the "not proof" disclaimer attached to every card, never truncated.

The guardrail that makes "low FP" hold at the ring level too

A popular merchant or payee is touched by hundreds of reports. Left alone it would fuse every unrelated report that names it into one giant false "ring". The hub-degree cap prunes any identifier with fan-in above the cap, so fan-in ≠ proof — the same principle that keeps a busy corridor or a shared channel word off the proof surface.

Component 03 · the evidence layer

Prove

Every flagged ring exports an evidence pack built for admissibility rather than for a demo screenshot. It itemises which reports share which identifiers, when, and how the ring was derived — and it is regenerable byte-for-byte from the same report set.

What ships in the pack
  • Per-incident SHA-256 of each raw report, plus a pack-level content hash (a derived property, so it can never drift from the content).
  • An itemised "shared identifiers" list — only identifiers named by ≥2 incidents of the ring, i.e. the values that actually form an edge.
  • A methodology block stamped with the ring's actual detection parameters, read off the ring itself so a caller cannot misstate them.
  • A real code_version (9f7af0f) and a Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam 2023 §63 certificate placeholder.
Why the two-layer split is the differentiator

The auditability criterion is answered by refusing to blur proof and lead. Layer 1 is deterministic structure a defence can re-run and verify. Layer 2 is a centrality score that is honestly labelled a prioritisation lead. Nothing in the pack presents the AI ranking as evidence — that discipline is the point.

Guardrails kept: legit-high-degree hub test, one adversarial device-rotation case, and a live precision/recall threshold slider in the demo.

Not crying wolf

Three guardrails, because "low false positive" is a promise

A citizen-facing tool that over-flags is worse than none — it trains people to ignore it and it can smear the innocent. The false-positive discipline is enforced in three concrete places, each one live in the demo rather than asserted on a slide.

01 · Legit high-degree hub

Popular ≠ a ring

A busy merchant UPI is named by hundreds of unrelated reports. Left alone it would fuse them all into one giant false ring. The hub-degree cap prunes any identifier with fan-in above the cap, so fan-in is never proof. Tested: the popular payee is explicitly not flagged, and never lands on the evidence surface.

02 · Adversarial device rotation

One fraudster, many handsets

A fraudster who splits activity across rotating devices to dodge clustering is the honest limitation, stated on screen: while the shared device's fan-in exceeds the cap it is capped out, so Layer 1 links on the durable identifiers (payee, account) instead. The guardrail reports what actually happened — its detail string can't overstate the case.

03 · Threshold slider

Precision vs recall, live

The hub-degree cap is a slider in the demo, so a judge can watch the precision/recall trade-off move in real time. Both failure ends are visible — too strict prunes real rings, too loose re-admits the hub — which is what makes the perfect plateau in the middle believable.

The sharper version of the question, answered

"What stops someone spam-reporting an innocent merchant to frame them?" A single report cannot create a ring — detect_rings requires at least two independent incidents sharing a hard identifier before anything is shown as a cluster. And even a real Layer 1 cluster is an investigative lead for a human, not an automated action. Reporter provenance and per-reporter rate-limits are the stated next hardening step — the corroboration bar sits on the identifier graph today, a second bar on the reporter graph is production.

Trust · the quiet superweapon

Because the network is planted, we can prove the answer

The synthetic network carries a known answer key — which reports belong to which ring, and which node is the seeded controller. That is not a shortcut; it is the same controlled-injection methodology the reused engine is built on, and it lets us show a real precision/recall curve and prove the kingpin ranking is correct — a claim no team using only scraped data can make.

Ring recovery vs the hub-degree cap · seed 0 · the threshold slider, as a curve
precision recall both ends fail visibly — that is what shows the 1.0s in the middle aren't rigged

Too strict (cap 5–20) and the cap prunes the bigger rings' own mule UPIs, so recall collapses. Too loose (cap 60 → re-admits the kingpin phone; 80 → the merchants; uncapped → the legit hub) and precision falls in visible stages. The plateau at 30–50 is real, not asserted — and the demo cap of 40 sits inside it with headroom for live reports.

MetricValuePosture
Kingpin ranked #120 / 20 seedsindependently seeded networks
Ring precision1.00 ± 0.00across all 20 seeds
Ring recall1.00 ± 0.00across all 20 seeds
Rings recovered per seed6 / 6at hub-degree cap 40
Say it out loud

Planted rings are clean by design. The claim is "the method recovers the answer key exactly, across 20 independent draws" — not "1.0 precision on real-world data". The real-world number is the false-positive rate on UCI and NUS. Stating the caveat is what makes the 1.0s worth believing.

The counterfactual

Lead time before mass victimisation

Replayed from the real report arrival order — not asserted. The reports of ring R0000 are fed in the order they actually arrived; the graph is rebuilt after each one and the ring is re-detected.

report 2
The ring flags on its second report — two victims naming one mule is enough
28
Subsequent victims were preventable (of 30 total)
₹32.70 L
89.7% of the ring's ₹36.45 L loss arrived after the flag
Counterfeit currency · FICN

The same spine, re-pointed to seizure records

The rubric names counterfeit detection as a scored criterion. Rather than fake a note-image accuracy number, the intelligence half is built for real: the entire Detect→Link→Prove spine is re-pointed to fake-currency circulation, changing only the schema roles. Seizures that share a reused printing-plate serial cluster into a circulation ring; the courier account laundering proceeds across several print operations is capped out of Layer 1 and surfaces as the Layer 2 kingpin; the same hash-stamped §63 pack is emitted.

1.0 / 1.0
Ring recovery P/R across 20 seeds (± 0.0)
20 / 20
Courier kingpin #1 on every seed
§63
Same BSA 2023 evidence pack · hash 16ca4d66…
Honest scope · stated, not faked

This is not a note-image counterfeit classifier. A real "accuracy across denominations and print quality" figure needs a labelled genuine-vs-fake image dataset we will not fabricate, so it is stated as out of scope rather than faked — the same answer-key-recovery claim and caveat as the scam side, not a zero pretending to be a build.

Does it scale?

50,600 reports, end to end, in 18 seconds

Benchmarked from generation through graph build, ring detection and kingpin ranking. The intelligence steps themselves stay well under a second even at fifty thousand reports — the bulk of the wall-clock is synthetic generation, which production would not do.

Full-pipeline wall-clock vs report count · hub-degree cap 40
524reports
0.28 s
1,080reports
0.47 s
5,200reports
2.01 s
20,400reports
7.48 s
50,600reports
18.12 s

At 50,600 reports, ring detection is 0.85 s and kingpin ranking 0.12 s; the rest is generation. Detection latency is not the bottleneck at demo or municipal scale.

Foundations

What we reused, what we measured on, what we built with

Reused, not reinvented — the cross-border-fraud engine

The graph intelligence is not written from scratch. A proven GraphSAGE ring engine with a controlled-injection answer key is vendored in, and the only adaptation is re-pointing its Schema roles. That same re-point is done three times — card transactions → scam incidents → FICN seizures — which is exactly why the counterfeit domain was a configuration change, not a rebuild.

Engine primitiveReused forAdaptation
Schema (role → column map)node/edge role mappingnew instance: entity = payee/account, target = victim/merchant, time = timestamp
inject_ring + answer keysynthetic network with planted rings + kingpinre-point roles; plant a cross-ring central node
RingSAGE (windowed fan-in)ring clustering / embeddingvendored; kept out of the demo path (Layer 1 is deliberately deterministic)
controlled-injection methodologythe whole trust storytransfers directly — it is how the answer key exists to validate against

Datasets — and why synthetic is the honest choice

Real victim data is legally radioactive, and a hackathon graph needs volume no live demo can supply. Planting typed signatures with an answer key gives ground-truth to validate against; real corpora ground the false-positive claim. The caveat is always stated plainly.

DatasetRoleSourceReal / synthetic
Synthetic scam corpus (answer-key)detector + the report bridgegenerated, src/generate/synthetic (controlled)
Synthetic scam network (planted kingpin)graph + evidenceengine inject.pysynthetic (controlled)
UCI SMS Spam Collectionreal ham/spam FP groundingUCI / Kaggle uciml/sms-spam-collection-datasetreal text
NUS SMS Corpus (English)held-out real ham evalGitHub WING-NUS/nus-sms-corpusreal text
PaySimmule money-movement substrateKaggle ealaxi/paysim1simulated

NUS SMS Corpus citation, per its research-use terms: Tao Chen and Min-Yen Kan (2013), "Creating a Live, Public Short Message Service Corpus: The NUS SMS Corpus," Language Resources and Evaluation 47(2), 299–355.

Tech stack — deliberately small, all pinned

What runs the pipeline
  • Detect — Groq LLM (llama-3.3-70b-versatile) + regex/rules floor.
  • Link — networkx for graph build and centrality.
  • Prove — reportlab (PDF), hashlib (SHA-256), stdlib.
  • Data — numpy / pandas + the vendored injector.
What runs the demo
  • App — Streamlit, single process, offline-safe.
  • Graph viz — pyvis, physics frozen after stabilise.
  • Charts — altair, themed to the graph clusters.
  • Runtime — 11 pinned deps, all imported, no torch in the demo path.
The discipline

Every figure in this report is produced by code in the repo

There are no asserted numbers. Each metric above is read from a code-produced artifact under data/processed/, regenerable by one command, and guarded by a test.

ClaimRegenerate withArtifact
Ring P/R + kingpin #1 + slider curvepython -m src.evidence.validatevalidation.json
Digital-arrest P/R (rules + LLM)python -m src.detector.eval_digital_arrestdigital_arrest_eval.json
UCI real-ham FP ratepython -m src.detector.eval_uciuci_eval.json
NUS held-out FP ratepython -m src.detector.eval_nusnus_eval.json
Counterfeit ring P/R + courier #1python -m src.counterfeit.validateficn_validation.json
Pipeline latency vs scalepython -m src.evidence.scale_benchmarkscale_benchmark.json

Why synthetic is the honest choice, not a shortcut: real victim data is legally radioactive, and a hackathon graph needs volume no live demo can supply. Planting typed signatures with an answer key gives ground-truth to validate against — the same method the reused engine is built on. The caveat is always stated plainly.

Engineering record

Built, audited, and pinned

246
Tests green in 62 s, offline on the rules floor
26
App-layer tests on both classifier paths
8
Audit rounds — every bug reproduced before fixed
11
Runtime deps, all pinned, all imported, no torch
What the audits caught

Twenty-two named pipeline bugs (B1–B22) and an app-layer series (A1–A11, T1), each reproduced before it was fixed and each pinned by a regression test. The pattern most worth noting: several were the same mistake — an identifier counted as linking when it links nothing (an email matched as a UPI, a channel word as a device, the legit hub credited with a link, a single-incident identifier itemised as "shared"). Every one was on the proof surface, and every one is now guarded.

Provenance you cannot misstate

The evidence pack's methodology block reads its detection parameters off the ring, set by the only code that detects rings — so a caller cannot certify a hub cap the ring was never found with. Provenance is structural, not remembered. The code_version stamp is a real commit hash now that the repo has history.

  • ✓Reused, not reinvented. The GraphSAGE ring engine and its controlled-injection answer key are vendored from the cross-border-fraud engine; only the schema roles are re-pointed. The source engine is never edited.
  • ✓Offline-safe by design. No API key, no internet → the deterministic rules floor runs the whole demo path. That is not a fallback bolted on; it is the configuration the suite runs in.
  • ✓Clean-venv verified. A fresh environment from the pinned requirements.txt (80 packages, no torch) runs the full demo path and serves the app — verified, not asserted.
  • ✓Design system, not stock widgets. The app is unified onto one WhatsApp-console design system; every chart reads real pipeline output and is themed to match the graph clusters.
The working prototype

Four pages, one console

A Streamlit app in the app's own dark WhatsApp-console identity, verified the way a judge sees it: headless-browser screenshot passes are a required step before any freeze, because string-asserting tests cannot see a layout. Every screenshot in this report is a real render of the running app.

Pages
  • Live — chat + network on one screen, the pipeline strip lighting up as the demo reaches each stage.
  • Command centre — network, kingpins, evidence pack, and the measured validation numbers, with the hub-cap slider.
  • Counterfeit — the FICN spine demonstrated live: circulation network, courier kingpin, §63 pack.
  • Lead time — the counterfactual as motion: reports arrive, the ring flags at report 2, the rest keep arriving in red.
The fold is measured

The Live-page kingpin banner — the two-layer payoff — is held above the fold at y ≈ 971, so the reveal lands without scrolling. The validation numbers shown in-app are read from the same code-produced artifacts as this report, never typed into the page.

Run it: streamlit run app/streamlit_app.py — fully functional with no API key and no internet.

The demo · five beats

How it presents in three to four minutes

The hero moment is the kingpin reveal, driven by a live citizen report. The citizen catch is the emotional setup; the payoff is "this isn't one scammer — it's a ring of N, and here's the boss." The live message is staged so its extracted payee matches a seeded node — legitimate staging, because it demonstrates the real mechanic (a new report joining a known network) deterministically.

#BeatOn screen
1The hookA citizen forwards a digital-arrest message — CBI, a laundering case, a warrant, pay now. ₹1,776 crore was lost to exactly this in nine months.
2DetectVerdict digital arrest · 0.99; five red flags light; guidance "Don't pay. Report 1930." And it quietly emits a structured report.
3Link — the revealThe report snaps into ring R0000, which goes 30 → 31 incidents, ₹36.95 L. "Why it linked: mule00@okaxis — we've seen it thirty times."
4KingpinThe phone in the message they just read is central across R0000 / R0001 / R0002 — one controller, three scams. Layer 1 refuses to link on it (too high fan-in); it's a lead, not proof. And because the network is planted, the #1 is the seeded controller — 20/20 draws.
5ProveThe evidence pack opens: Layer-1 hard-connection subgraph + Layer-2 lead. Drag the threshold slider — precision vs recall, live. The legit hub, popular and high-degree, is not flagged.

Closer: open the Lead time page and scrub — "flagged at report 2; 28 subsequent victims were preventable, ₹32.70 lakh of them" — about the same ring R0000 the live report just joined. Every figure spoken is produced by code, never a number on a slide.

Anticipated questions

The hard questions, answered straight

Improvised answers to hostile questions read as improvised. These are the ones a sharp judge asks, with the honest posture each deserves — the boundary owned, not bluffed.

Q

Counterfeit detection is first on your evaluation focus and you don't do it. Why not mark that a zero?

A

We won't pretend a number we didn't measure — every figure on the trust slide is code-produced, and a real counterfeit-CV accuracy "across denominations and print quality" needs a labelled genuine-vs-fake note dataset we don't have and won't fabricate. Faking that one number would cost the credibility of the other four.

But the intelligence half of counterfeiting we do answer, and we built it. Fake-currency circulation is a network — the same serial resurfacing at different seizures, the couriers who move it, the accounts that bank it. src/counterfeit/ re-points the engine's Schema to FICN roles and runs the identical Layer 1 / Layer 2 / evidence-pack spine: ring recovery 1.0 over twenty seeds, courier ranked #1 in 20/20. Deep and provable on four and a half of five, honest about the CV half, beats shallow and asserted on all five.

Q

What stops someone spam-reporting an innocent merchant's UPI to frame them?

A

A single report can't create a ring — detect_rings requires at least two independent incidents sharing a hard identifier before anything is even shown as a cluster. One hostile report against a merchant with no prior history produces nothing, not an accusation. And a real cluster is scoped as an investigative lead for a human, never an automated action.

The honest residual: two colluding reporters clear min_incidents=2 today — this repo scores identifier co-occurrence, not reporter trust. Reporter provenance and per-reporter rate-limits are the stated next hardening step, which is exactly why Layer 1 is "evidence for a human", not "verdict".

Q

Is this DPDP Act 2023 compliant? You're processing phone numbers, UPI IDs, financial identifiers.

A

Two flows, two grounds. The citizen's own report — text forwarded voluntarily to check a scam — sits in Section 7(a), "certain legitimate use". The harder leg is the other identifiers inside it (a scammer's or a merchant's UPI, from someone who gave no consent): Section 17(1)(c) exempts processing for prevention/detection/investigation of an offence — which is what ring detection is — though that exemption is narrow and only holds where there's real reason to believe an offence occurred, not a bare accusation. Which is why a Layer 1 cluster never accuses anyone on its own.

Production gap, stated: nothing here is a registered Data Fiduciary today — no consent-manager, no rights portal, no retention timer. In practice this sits behind 1930 / NCRP as the Fiduciary of record.

Q

What about Hindi in Devanagari script, not just Hinglish?

A

The rules floor is romanized-only today — the Hinglish keywords are Latin-script ("turant", "bhej do"), matching how most Indian scam SMS is actually typed. Native Devanagari won't fire a keyword on the rules floor; the LLM path handles it fine, but the rules floor is the one guaranteed to run with no API key, and that's where the gap sits. It's a scoped, known limitation — the fix is a transliteration step or a parallel Devanagari table before the app door, not a redesign.

Q

This demo runs on a few hundred reports. Does any of it actually scale?

A

We measured it instead of guessing. The scale benchmark sweeps up to 50,600 reports and 150 planted rings and times the three stages that run live. At that size, ring detection is 0.85 s and kingpin ranking 0.12 s — about a second combined, on a laptop, no GPU — and recovery stays exact at every scale. The slow part is generating fifty thousand synthetic reports to test against, which production never pays; live reports arrive one at a time. Centrality runs on the ring-union graph, so it grows with the number of rings, not with legit traffic volume.

Honest boundaries

What is demonstrated, and what production would still take

Demonstrated
  • The full Detect→Link→Prove pipeline on both scam and counterfeit domains.
  • Real-data false-positive rates on two independent human-SMS corpora.
  • Answer-key ring recovery and kingpin ranking, with the caveat stated.
  • A hash-stamped, §63-framed evidence pack with structural provenance.
Production would take
  • Real ingest and a signed §63 certificate, replacing the placeholder block.
  • Integration with 1930 / NCRP report intake.
  • A labelled genuine-vs-fake note-image corpus for the counterfeit CV line — deliberately out of scope here rather than faked.
  • Human-in-the-loop review on every Layer 2 lead before any action.

Night is gathering, and now my watch begins.
Detect the scam. Link the ring. Prove the case.
